programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits America's most interesting and unique restaurants.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri's going for amped-up pork tacos in Sacramento, CA, and a BBQ joint in Palm Beach Gardens, FL, is bringing brisket to a stacked sloppy joe. Plus, a real-deal Cambodian spot in San Jose, CA, is serving primo pork belly noodles.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Viewer favorites.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits America's most interesting and unique restaurants. Guy finds fresh crab in San Francisco, homemade granola in Nevada, and boiled turkey in North Carolina.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Adam heads to Chicago, where a plate of overstuffed sandwiches with fries is awaiting him.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Atlanta is the next stop on Adams culinary tour, where he attempts to finish a 13-pound Carnivore Pizza. However, this is one challenge he'll need some help with.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Adam takes on the hottest curry in the country at New York's Brick Lane Curry House.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Adam heads to Portland, Oregon where a habanero fritter challenge await him.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: In St Louis, Adam attempts to down almost a gallon of the best milkshake in town.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Adam faces a hot task as he tries to consume a plate of Atomic Hot Wings in Pittsburgh.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: The next stop on Adam Richman's culinary tour of the United States is Boston, where he encounters the giant Eagle's Challenge Burger. Does it come with a giant soda?

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Adam Richman heads to New Orleans, where he takes on a plate of 15 dozen oysters at Acme's Oyster House. Did he remember to bring a knife?

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Adam tackles a five pound omelette made with 12 eggs.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: How will Adam fare when he faces a spicy ramen made with ten different chilies?

programme.title: James Martin's Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: On the next leg of his UK tour, James is joined in glorious Devon by his friend and Michelin-starred chef Simon Hulstone. James visits an extraordinary mushroom farm situated in a bungalow, and makes a tasty steak and mushroom dish. Simon samples some award-winning cheese, which inspires James to make a cheese platter fit for a king. The day ends with a trip to the Art Deco splendour of Burgh Island.

programme.title: James Martin's Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James is joined by pal and Michelin-starred chef Mark Jordan, and together they set out to explore the delights of Jersey aboard mini motor bikes. James samples the delights of one of Jersey's most famous exports, the Jersey Royal potato, and he uses them to cook up a squid risotto with a difference. Mark Jordan gets a lesson in oyster shucking, and James discovers an island speciality: Jersey black butter, which he uses to create a delicious sweet treat.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: This time the judges set challenges inspired by the roaring twenties, including a slapstick Signature and a tiered Showstopper offering a twist on their favourite tipple.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: The cake layers stack up in an elegant and challenging bake for Dessert Week. There's also a spherical celebration cake and a Technical where success lies in precise decoration.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: It's Festival Week as the bakers draw inspiration from around the world, including something deep fried and Italian, and a spectacular Indonesian celebration cake.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: The remaining bakers have their knowledge of pastry tested to the max with a savoury take on a French classic, a multi-layered Moroccan Technical, and a towering Showstopper.

programme.title: James Martin's Spanish Adventure
programme.desc: James' adventure begins in San Sebastian, a city boasting 16 Michelin stars - he experiences pintxos, tries whole-grilled turbot, samples local cider and shops at the fish market while also cooking hake, lamb, Basque stew and pintxos.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Adam must eat 12 of San Jose's hottest hot wings without taking a drink - for five minutes after the food is gone.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: In Denver, Adam tackles a giant breakfast burrito containing seven potatoes, a pound of ham and 12 eggs.

programme.title: James Martin's Great British Adventure
programme.desc: James Martin's British adventure travels as far to the east of England as this journey will go, to Norfolk, where he is joined by his good old mate and top local chef, Galton Blackiston. They meet in Cromer, where Galton has a successful fish and chip shop, which inspires James to cook pan-fried cod with a warm tartar sauce on the beach. Then James heads to Norfolk Lavender to find out why it grows so well in this county and how it's distilled for oil.

programme.title: James Martin's Great British Adventure
programme.desc: No adventure around the UK would be complete without a trip to the capital city, London. James is checking out some of his old haunts and stopping in on Clare Smyth's brand new restaurant Core for a bite to eat and a catch up with the world's best female chef. Then high up on the rooftops of London, he cooks up spiced pigeon breast with dhal. Next, James ventures into south east London to a knife forgery in Peckham. He gets hands on in the process of making Japanese style knives.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri's going for amped-up pork tacos in Sacramento, CA, and a BBQ joint in Palm Beach Gardens, FL, is bringing brisket to a stacked sloppy joe. Plus, a real-deal Cambodian spot in San Jose, CA, is serving primo pork belly noodles.

programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits America's most interesting and unique restaurants.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Handmade patties with fried green tomatoes; homemade bread; scrapple.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits America's most interesting and unique restaurants. In Hawaii Guy goes to a water side spot with a real catch of the day, a hot dog place with lobster dogs and a luau with roasted pig.

programme.title: River Cottage: Road Trip
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall's journey begins in Dumfriesshire, where he learns how to make haggis.

programme.title: Mary Berry Everyday
programme.desc: Mary shares some inspirational ideas to make everyday family meals exciting with fabulous no fuss dishes. She tries her hand at milking a goat and shows her granddaughter how to make her own goats cheese for a family picnic.

programme.title: Mary Berry Everyday
programme.sub-title: When You Want to Show Off
programme.desc: As Mary and her husband reach their 50th wedding anniversary it's time to push the boat out. Mary shares her secrets to surprisingly easy showstoppers, recreates canapés from the 1960s and finishes the series with sparkle.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: On the menu are divine ham and leak mini quiches, garden spread finger sandwiches, lemon raspberry tarts, mini doughnut muffins and fancy hot chocolate.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: It's boxing night, so Ree's running her sons to town for practice. She bakes them fruity granola chocolate chip cookies and prepares a knockout dinner of Italian sloppy joes, garlic toast and a fabulous lemon meringue pie.

programme.title: Jamie's 15 Minute Meals
programme.desc: Jamie rustles up seared Asian tuna, coconut rice and jiggy jiggy greens, plus ricotta fritters, tomato sauce and courgette salad.

programme.title: Jamie's 15 Minute Meals
programme.sub-title: Falafel Wraps and Spiced Chicken Lentils
programme.desc: This time the meal is spiced chicken, bacon, asparagus and spinach lentils, plus falafel wraps, grilled veg and salsa.

programme.title: Be My Guest With Ina Garten
programme.desc: The Emmy-winning actor, comedian, writer and producer Julia Louis-Dreyfus joins Ina Garten at the barn to enjoy country French omelettes and a Beatty's chocolate cake frosting and piping masterclass.

programme.title: Be My Guest With Ina Garten
programme.desc: Stephen Colbert and Evie McGee-Colbert visit Ina Garten for sour cream coffee cake and to chat about working in live TV. The Colberts whip up two recipes from their new cookbook: green goddess dip and Capri sunset cocktails.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: The remaining bakers take on more challenges set by judges Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith in the nation's most famous tent, in a bid to be crowned star baker.

programme.title: Rachel Allen: Bake!
programme.desc: On the menu: a gorgeous coconut macaroon tart. Plus, Rachel meets master patissier Eric Lanlard, and learns the secrets behind his flamboyant creations.

programme.title: James Martin's Great British Adventure
programme.desc: James and Paul are having a blast in Northern Ireland and they've reached Paul's home town of Belfast, where he's giving James a personal tour of Northern Ireland's capital city before they head off for a spot of golf. James pitches up his mobile kitchen right on the green to whip up a tasty dish with bacon, scallops and apples with onion rings. After all his exertion on the golf course, Paul heads just out of town to Carnbrooke Meats.

programme.title: James Martin's Great British Adventure
programme.desc: Following his French and American Adventures, James Martin explores the British culinary offerings.

programme.title: Mary Berry Everyday
programme.desc: Mary shares some inspirational ideas to make everyday family meals exciting with fabulous no fuss dishes. She tries her hand at milking a goat and shows her granddaughter how to make her own goats cheese for a family picnic.

programme.title: Mary Berry Everyday
programme.sub-title: When You Want to Show Off
programme.desc: As Mary and her husband reach their 50th wedding anniversary it's time to push the boat out. Mary shares her secrets to surprisingly easy showstoppers, recreates canapés from the 1960s and finishes the series with sparkle.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: On the menu are divine ham and leak mini quiches, garden spread finger sandwiches, lemon raspberry tarts, mini doughnut muffins and fancy hot chocolate.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: It's boxing night, so Ree's running her sons to town for practice. She bakes them fruity granola chocolate chip cookies and prepares a knockout dinner of Italian sloppy joes, garlic toast and a fabulous lemon meringue pie.

programme.title: Jamie's 15 Minute Meals
programme.desc: Jamie rustles up seared Asian tuna, coconut rice and jiggy jiggy greens, plus ricotta fritters, tomato sauce and courgette salad.

programme.title: Jamie's 15 Minute Meals
programme.sub-title: Falafel Wraps and Spiced Chicken Lentils
programme.desc: This time the meal is spiced chicken, bacon, asparagus and spinach lentils, plus falafel wraps, grilled veg and salsa.

programme.title: Be My Guest With Ina Garten
programme.desc: Tina Fey joins Ina Garten for a fun-filled day of deep conversation and cooking. Ina welcomes Tina with Breakfast Panna Cotta, and they team up to make Tina's signature Greek Lemon Potatoes served with Ina's Chicken Thighs with Creamy Mustard Sauce.

programme.title: Be My Guest With Ina Garten
programme.desc: Ina Garten welcomes old and new friends into her home for good food, great conversation and lots of fun. Ina prepares delicious treats for her guests, and they share their amazing life stories, along with some of their own signature dishes.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: The remaining bakers take on more challenges set by judges Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith in the nation's most famous tent, in a bid to be crowned star baker.

programme.title: Rachel Allen: Bake!
programme.desc: On the menu: a gorgeous coconut macaroon tart. Plus, Rachel meets master patissier Eric Lanlard, and learns the secrets behind his flamboyant creations.

programme.title: James Martin's Spanish Adventure
programme.desc: In this episode, James visits a huge indoor food market in Bilbao, takes a funicular to a famous steak restaurant, and heads to a winery on a seabed - we also see how La Carolina is made as James cooks cod, squid ink arancini, Basque cheesecake and seafood on a boat.

programme.title: James Martin's Spanish Adventure
programme.desc: In this episode, James meets a family who live off-grid in Asturias, samples fabada stew and Urbies cheese, and visits a restaurant famous for its sardines - James also cooks a steak sandwich, pancetta and clams in red wine, rice pudding, and sardines with Padron peppers.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits America's most interesting and unique restaurants. In Honolulu Guy has a drive-in burger, a deli in Minnesota serves fish to go and a Los Angeles truck sells Chinese-Mexican food.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: In Kentucky Guy visits a sandwich joint with home made bread, tries Cajun coastal flavours in Georgia, and pizza in Washington, DC.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy is on a Kansas City barbeque tour and tries smoked pork, deep fried barbecued chicken and baby back ribs.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits America's most interesting and unique restaurants. Guy visits a pub in Kansas City for tempura burgers, a cold cut burger spot in California, and has Greek lamb burgers in Nashville.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Adam must eat 12 of San Jose's hottest hot wings without taking a drink - for five minutes after the food is gone.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: In Denver, Adam tackles a giant breakfast burrito containing seven potatoes, a pound of ham and 12 eggs.

programme.title: Cake Boss
programme.desc: Mauro makes a Korean-themed first birthday cake, Madeline picks a dress for her 25th wedding anniversary and Buddy is tasked with a bake for a digger theme park!

programme.title: Cake Boss
programme.desc: Buddy judges a true `clash-of-the-titans' - a cook-off between local police and fire-fighters, complete with two fabulous cakes to celebrate the event!

programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's decision time on Great British Menu for the chefs from the South West region. One chef left the competition on the previous day and only two remain to cook their entire menus for the `Great British Menu' judges, Prue Leith, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton. The judges aren't easily pleased and only world class cooking will do.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The 2012 competition enters its final week. The winners of the regional heats compete for a chance for their dish to be on the final banquet menu at a prestigious event hosted by Sir Steve Redgrave with a glittering guest list of British Olympians. Here, the finalists cook their starters, which will be tasted and scored by the `Great British Menu' judges and veteran judge Richard Corrigan.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The eight winners of the regional heats prepare to cook their fish dishes, which will be tasted and scored by the `Great British Menu' judges and veteran judge Marcus Wareing. Before they get into the kitchen, they must first learn whether they will all be cooking.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: On day three, the chosen finalists cook their main courses, which will be tasted and scored by the `Great British Menu' judges and veteran judge Tom Kerridge.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's desserts day in the finals, and the last chance for the competing chefs to impress the judges and veteran Angela Hartnett. After a hard-fought week, the end of this programme will see the judges finally reveal the winning four dishes which will make up this year's `Great British Menu'.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: This final programme follows the successful chefs in the run-up to the Olympic banquet at the Old Royal Naval College in Greenwich, as they make the painstaking preparations for what is one of the most important meals they will ever cook. The four winning chefs prepare an outstanding four-course menu for a truly Olympian guest list.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The spotlight is on Scotland. Returning contenders Michael Smith, Tony Singh and Mark Greenaway are all determined to get one of their dishes to the banquet. Will a witty take on spiced ham score highly, or will a warm chicken salad with a twist or a rabbit terrine come out on top? Veteran judge Angela Hartnett MBE will be judging.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Returning contenders Michael Smith, Tony Singh and Mark Greenaway cook their fish dishes. Will a traditional Scottish soup, a chowder or a risky no-cook mackerel dish impress veteran judge Angela Hartnett MBE?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The competition heats up as the three returning Scottish contenders, Michael Smith, Tony Singh and Mark Greenaway, cook their main courses. An African inspired goat tagine, a beef dish inspired by a childhood cartoon and a duck dish with a comedy twist are dished up for veteran judge Angela Hartnett MBE.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Scottish returning contenders Michael Smith, Tony Singh and Mark Greenaway have just one more chance to impress veteran judge Angela Hartnett MBE and win their place in front of the judges. Will a comedy custard pie, a sophisticated chocolate dessert or doughnuts inspired by a Scottish tradition come out on top?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Only one chef can make it through to the national finals to represent Scotland and cook at a special banquet at the Royal Albert Hall.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The curtain raises on the competition in the North West. How will Aiden Byrne's dish entitled `Mad as a Box of Frogs' go down with veteran Phil Howard? Will he trounce Mary-Ellen McTague's crab starter or will Chris Holland's `Oriental style beef broth with a twis' score more highly with Phil?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The North West chefs cook their fish courses. Will Aiden Byrne's `The Culinary Joke' - a take on a classic prawn cocktail - raise a laugh, or will Mary-Ellen McTague's dish of dover sole or Chris Holland's `Gone Fishing' salmon dish prove winners?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs cook their main course. Will Aiden Byrne's witty beef dish entitled `Long Horn Beef with Noodle Pot' prove a winner, or will Mary-Ellen McTague's goat dish called `Our Kid' raise a smile? Perhaps Chris Holland will score highly with his dish called `A Tasting of Pork'.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the chef's last chance to impress before the final two are chosen to face the `Great British Menu' judging panel. Which dessert will help get them through? Will it be Aiden Byrne's pineapple dish called `The Sweet Burger', Mary-Ellen McTague's take on a trifle called `Chamomile Lawn' or Chris Holland's dish called `Going Bananas'?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's decision time for the chefs from the North West. One chef has already left the competition and only two remain. The judges are joined by celebrity guest judge writer, presenter and performer Rowland Rivron.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: In central England, three culinary heavyweights return to the `Great British Menu' kitchen. Two Michelin star chef Daniel Clifford creates a dish called `A Trip to the Chippy', where nothing is as it first appears. That is also the theme for Michelin star chef Richard Bainbridge's starter, `banana split', while Will Holland offers up goats cheese mousse, balsamic jelly and gingerbread.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Here, the chefs try to impress with their fish courses. Daniel Clifford puts up a creative dish called `Catch of the Day'. Does Richard Bainbridge's dish titled `Tongue in Cheek' live up to its comedy name? Will judge Marcus Wareing find the comedy value in Will Holland's coconut poached brill, crab, celeriac and tomato essence?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs in central England cook their main courses. Daniel Clifford's main has the same title as Richard Bainbridge's fish course, `Tongue in Cheek', but it is a wildly different plate of food; Richard Bainbridge presents guinea fowl in a beef bladder and calls the dish `Which Came First: the Chicken or the Egg'; Will Holland offers roast longhorn beef rib eye, bread and bone marrow pudding and parsley paste.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs in central England have one last chance to impress Marcus Wareing before the final two are chosen to face the judging panel. Will Daniel Clifford's `Going Out with a Bang' be good enough? Has Richard Bainbridge dropped the comedy ball with his coffee and chocolate dessert called `Time to Reflect'? Or will an unusual dish of parsnip banoffee cake, chocolate tart and parsnip ice cream get Will Holland through?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's decision time for the chefs from the central England region. Only two chefs remain to cook their entire menus for judges Prue Leith, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton. The panel is joined by guest judge, comedian and actor Jim Moir, known to millions as Vic Reeves.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The South West chefs battle it out for a chance to cook at the special banquet. Will veteran judge Tom Kerridge like Chris Eden's modern twist on `Duck a l'Orange', or will he favour Peter Sanchez-Iglesias' dish called `Picnic'? Perhaps Emily Watkins's witty dish called `Eggs and Ham' will prove a winner.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The South West chefs cook their fish courses here. Cornishman Chris Eden takes inspiration from home to create a lobster and oyster dish called `Gone Surfing', Peter Sanchez-Iglesias cooks up a dish inspired by childhood memories titled `Fish and Chips', while Emily Watkins serves up a dish with cured trout called `High Tea'.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The South West chefs cook their main courses and veteran Tom Kerridge is looking for perfection. Will Chris Eden's spin on a fast food classic simply called `B.L.T' gain top points? Or will Peter Sanchez Iglesias' nostalgic dish `Roast Dinner' or Emily Watkins' venison dish `Shoot Lunch' prove a winner?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: For the South West chefs, which dessert will progress? Peach melba, apple pie and `Lolly and Sweets' fight it out.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's decision time for the chefs from the South West and only two remain. Who will cook their entire menus for the `Great British Menu' judges, Prue Leith, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton and their guest, writer and performer Simon Day? The judges aren't easily pleased and only world-class cooking will do.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The Welsh chefs raise the curtain with their starters, but which one is going to produce a showstopper? All three contestants have created sophisticated plates of food with simple dish titles. Will veteran Jeremy Lee be impressed with Richard Davies's chicken salad or will Mary Ann Gilchrist's peas and ham or Luke Thomas's ham, egg and pineapple get them off to a flying start?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The Welsh chefs cook their fish course. Richard Davies serves up grilled and tartare of mackerel, while Mary Ann Gilchrist serves a lobster dish and calls it `The Great Escape'. Luke Thomas is hoping that his quirky take on a classic fish finger will impress veteran Jeremy Lee too.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The Welsh chefs prepare their main courses, but will any of them impress veteran Jeremy Lee enough to give them a top score? Richard Davies's dish is simply called `A Tasting of Pork', Mary Ann Gilchrist's `Three's a Crowd' doesn't give much away, while Luke Thomas's dish is called `Beef, Bone Marrow and Watercress'.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the final chance for the Welsh chefs to impress Jeremy Lee with their dessert dishes. Which creation will help get two chefs through to the judging show? Will it be Richard Davies's strawberries and cream with a Comic Relief twist, or will Mary Ann Gilchrist's `Some Like It Hot' or Luke Thomas's rhubarb and custard get them through?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Great British Menu continues as 24 of the nation's finest chefs battle it out to cook gastronomic masterpieces that are also witty and fun. Their dishes have to put a smile on faces as they compete for the prize of cooking a dish at a special banquet at the Royal Albert Hall celebrating 25 years of Comic Relief's Red Nose Day appeals. It is decision time for the chefs representing Wales.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three North East chefs all want to top the leader board on day one with their starter. How will Colin McGurran's tomato gazpacho and garden salad perform? Stephanie Moon thinks she also has a chance with a witty chicken dish called `Why Did the Chicken...', and Charlie Lakin offers a controversial duck dish called `Mind Your Step'.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The North East chefs cook their fish courses. Will former champion Colin McGurran's dish of cockles and mussels with salmon, inspired by his childhood memories, also inspire veteran judge Jason Atherton? Or perhaps Stephanie Moon's fish dish with a nod to one of the most famous gags of all time, crab custard pie, will be the winner? Charlie Lakin is also confident with his creation called `Tongue in Cheek'.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The North East chefs cook their main course and veteran Jason Atherton is looking for perfection. Will Colin McGurran's dish of duck and turnips be funnier than it sounds? Has Stephanie Moon dropped the comedy ball with her venison dish called `Behind the Fun is Some Serious Game'? Will Charlie Lakin's kebab inspired dish `A Night on the Tiles' raise a smile?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the North East chefs' last chance to impress before the final two are chosen to face the `Great British Menu' judging panel. Which desserts will help get two chefs through to the showdown? Will it be Colin McGurran's tiramisu, Stephanie Moon's tricky pulled sugar dome, or Charlie Larkin's custard pie?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's decision time for the chefs from the North East. One chef has already left the competition and only two remain. They must now cook their entire menus for the `Great British Menu' judges Prue Leith, Matthew Fort, Oliver Peyton and their guest, comedian Tim Brooke-Taylor. The judges aren't easily pleased and only world class cooking will do.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The curtain raises on the competition in Northern Ireland. How will Chris Fearon cope competing against his former head chef Raymond McArdle? Will rising star Ian Orr be caught up in the middle? Both Chris and Raymond create very different takes on soup while Ian comes up with a rabbit dish.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The three Northern Ireland chefs cook their fish courses. Chris Fearon serves up a creation called `Stitched up Like a Kipper', while Raymond McArdle draws inspiration from a view from home and serves turbot called `A Day at the Beach'. Ian Orr creates a dish with a play on an old joke called `Why Did the Scallop Cross the Roe'.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's time for the main course. Chris Fearon's dish called `Cowboy Supper' is inspired by the Wild West. Raymond McArdle creates a dish called `Titanic Smoked Venison, Tongue and Cheek', and Ian Orr cooks lamb and baked hay.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's time for the main course. Chris Fearon's dish called `Cowboy Supper' is inspired by the Wild West. Raymond McArdle creates a dish called `Titanic Smoked Venison, Tongue and Cheek', and Ian Orr cooks lamb and baked hay.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the chefs' last chance to impress before the final two are chosen to face the `Great British Menu' judging panel. Which dessert will it be? Chris Fearon's dish inspired by comedy legend Tommy Cooper called `Just Like That?' competes against Raymond McArdle's `Chocolate Volcano Mountain' and Ian Orr's `Passion Egg'.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: In Norfolk, flint tools unearthed recently push the earliest human occupation back by 200,000 years, to around one million years ago. In Orkney an early farm yields glimpses of our ancestors' earliest religious beliefs and customs.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Skeletons tell of both violent death and tenderness at the royal seat of power in Bamburgh, Northumbria, while at a remarkable community project at a shopping centre in Sittingbourne, people curate the grave goods of their own ancestors.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Dr Alice Roberts follows an entire year of British archaeology, joining up the results of digs and investigations the length of the country. The results are astonishing, and sometimes disturbing. Roman finds include the mystery of 97 babies murdered by the Thames, a fabulous Roman coin hoard found in Somerset and a man buried on a layer of dead animals.

programme.title: Ross Kemp: Deep Sea Treasure Hunter
programme.desc: The quest continues in the Red Sea off the coast of Egypt, a deeply personal journey given Ross' grandfather fought in the North Africa campaign during World War II.

programme.title: Ross Kemp: Shipwreck Treasure Hunter
programme.desc: Inspired by the story of his two cousins that died at sea in WWII, Ross Kemp explores captured German scout warship, the F2 and brings up treasure that could link back to his relatives.

programme.title: Ross Kemp: Shipwreck Treasure Hunter
programme.desc: The Kaiser Sunken Fleet: Ross Kemp continues his adventure in Scapa Flow, plunging down on the wrecks of a German warship and an experimental submarine aircraft called the M2. (S1, ep 2)

programme.title: Ross Kemp: Shipwreck Treasure Hunter
programme.desc: Ross Kemp dives down on shipwrecks to uncover hidden stories, and recover treasure. He dives on warships, experimental submarines, early slaver ships and Napoleonic frigates, searching for clues and artefacts that reveal the story behind every wreck.

programme.title: Ross Kemp: Shipwreck Treasure Hunter
programme.desc: After hearing about his great-grandfather who served in the Merchant Navy, Ross Kemp is inspired to explore the wreck of a WWII cargo ship and try to recover its military supplies.

programme.title: Ross Kemp: Deep Sea Treasure Hunter
programme.desc: Ross heads to Portsmouth and the Mary Rose Museum and takes up an opportunity he can't turn down, to be one of the few divers allowed on the site of Britain's most famous wreck, the Mary Rose.

programme.title: Ross Kemp: Deep Sea Treasure Hunter
programme.desc: Ross hops round the coast from Portsmouth to Plymouth, then on to the Gulf of Suez. A bout of hypercapnia leaves Ross shaken after the dive of a 17th century shipwreck.

programme.title: Ross Kemp: Deep Sea Treasure Hunter
programme.desc: The quest continues in the Red Sea off the coast of Egypt, a deeply personal journey given Ross' grandfather fought in the North Africa campaign during World War II.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.desc: When a wardrobe malfunction occurs during a live broadcast, the FCC begins to censor all of Peter's favourite television shows. programme.title: American Dad!

programme.title: American Dad!

programme.title: The Only Way Is Essex
programme.desc: Having navigated their way through lockdown, the Essex elite are back and `TOWIE' royalty makes a welcome return to the fold. programme.desc: The ladies are unsure about how they feel about going on a trip with Monica for her birthday as Monica turns to Mary for advice. A lunch between Lisa and Meredith leaves their friendship on shaky ground.

programme.desc: When the Griffins visit Lois's parents, Lois realises that her father prevented her from fulfilling her lifelong dream of becoming a model, so she returns to Quahog and hits the catwalk. programme.title: Love Island: Aftersun
programme.desc: Love Island: Aftersun is back with the sensational Maya Jama kicking off another summer of love. Helping her to keep Love Island on everyone's lips are the big three: Shakira Khan, Toni Laites and Yasmin Pettet alongside Tyrique Hyde, Casey O'Gorman and Joe Baggs. programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When a wardrobe malfunction occurs during a live broadcast, the FCC begins to censor all of Peter's favourite television shows. Peter and Brian create their own edgy programming for the station until the FCC shows up to shut down PTV, and censor all controversial material in Quahog.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.sub-title: Breaking Out Is Hard to Do

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: When Steve brings home a permission slip to take sex education at school, Stan flips out and demands to teach it himself. Meanwhile, Roger becomes a bartender in hopes of making something of himself.

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: When Hayley decides to move to France because of imperialistic oppression, Stan will do anything to stop her, including revealing the fact that she was brainwashed as a child by the CIA. programme.title: G'Wed

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.sub-title: The Adventure of the Egyptian Tomb
programme.desc: It's 1935, and a team of international archaeologists in Egypt have broken the seal of the tomb of King Men-Her-Ra for the first time in 3000 years. Moments later, the leader of the group, Sir John Willard, drops down dead of a heart attack. Poirot is called upon to consider whether he is the victim of the tomb's legendary curse or whether there is a more mortal explanation for his death.

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Poirot is issued with a deadly personal challenge when a series of letters, mysteriously signed ABC, lead him to a string of brutal murders. Could Poirot be the next victim?

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Miss Marple is faced with her greatest challenge when she receives instructions from a recently deceased friend, Mr Rafiel, to investigate a possible crime. Miss Marple finds herself on a mystery coach tour with her nephew Raymond West and a number of intriguing passengers.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The worlds of miniature dolls houses and real houses collide when prolific real estate agent Alexander Beauvoisin is murdered in front of a crowd at the unveiling of a new dolls house collection at Midsomer Museum of the family.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The Deddington's thriving bee empire has put Granville Norton on the map, but the lengths they will go to to ensure they're never dethroned is extreme.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Fishermen and women flock to the village of Solomon Gorge, desperate to catch a giant fish that is said to be in the lake. Their plans are threatened however when hundreds of lycra-clad sporting enthusiasts descend on the area for the Psycho Mud Run.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.sub-title: The Wolf Hunter of Little Worthy
programme.desc: After a local photographer wins an urban myth competition with his creation of The Wolf Hunter, it unexpectedly gains a cult following. However, when a man is killed, Barnaby and Winter must investigate if this myth has become murderous reality.

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: When a woman is found killed in a remote cottage in the dramatic Northumberland countryside, DI Vera Stanhope is taken back to a place full of childhood memories - and an unsolved case from her own past. She and her team delve into the local politics surrounding a controversial proposal to dig a new quarry, and find motives for murder in the unlikeliest of places, but the truth could be hidden within the land itself.

programme.title: Protection

programme.desc: The 2026 British Touring Car Championship season continues at Oulton Park. programme.desc: The Road to 26 Series continues with a game between Morocco, who reached the World Cup semi-finals in Qatar in 2022, and Norway, who have not qualified for a World Cup since 1998. programme.title: World Cup Countdown
programme.desc: The countdown to the FIFA World Cup 2026 is on and Daniyal Khan, Cams Babayemi, Kweku Afari, Roy Keane, Ian Wright and the gang are giving a full rundown of what to expect ahead of the biggest tournament ever this summer. programme.title: The Isle of Man TT
programme.desc: Action from the second Carole Nash Sportbike TT race. Sportbike racing requires a very different riding style to that seen in the bigger classes, and losing speed on a Sportbike costs more than it does on a Superstock or Superbike machine. The riders who excel here are those who can stay disciplined over 113 miles.

programme.desc: For four seasons fans of `Teen Mom' watched Farrah, Maci, Amber and Catelynn face multiple challenges in their first years of motherhood, including such things as graduating from high school, relationships, getting jobs, beginning college and moving out on their own. Oh, and raising their first child. Now the original girls - hence, the `OG' in the revived series title - return to reveal the challenges of raising children on camera, and how life in the public eye has shaped them as parents and people. It's an unprecedented view of young mothers facing the responsibilities of parenthood as their children enter kindergarten, start little league and reach other milestones of youth.

programme.title: Ancient Greece: The Dark Chronicles

programme.title: Tomos a'i Ffrindiau

programme.title: Dal Dy Ddannedd

programme.title: Sam Tân

They are late so the three have to leave without them. Mandy is angry with Norman but she notices a rowing boat so she and Norman start looking for the turtle with Norman rowing. They get into difficulties when the weather changes. Will someone rescue them?

programme.title: Blwi
programme.desc: Mum and Dad construct their own magic claw machine at home for Bluey and Bingo to play with, but Dad makes sure winning isn't easy.

programme.title: Blero Yn Mynd I Ocido
programme.desc: Beth sy'n digwydd ym myd Blero heddiw? The events happening in Blero's world today.

programme.title: Kim a Cêt a Twrch
programme.desc: Ymunwch â Kim a Cêt ar antur hudolus sy'n llawn dawns a cherddoriaeth wrth iddyn nhw chwilio am Twrch yn y goedwig. Join Kim and Cêt as they search for the character Twrch in the woods.

programme.title: Sion Y Chef
programme.desc: Mama Polenta and Sam Spratt agree to a cook-off to determine who makes the best pasta sauce, but it's Izzy and her home-grown basil sauce that wins the day.

programme.title: Newyffion
programme.desc: Bu Newffion yn holi pobol ar y stryd pa iaith mae nhw'n siarad gyda'u cwn, ac hefyd yn holi awdur sydd wedi ysgrifennu llyfr am y pwnc. Today, we learn about a rare bird in Llanuwchllyn!

programme.title: Twm Twrch

programme.title: Y Fets

programme.title: Gwesty Aduniad
programme.desc: Help i Myra, 91, i ffeindio bedd ei brawd bach, sydd ar goll ar ochr arall y byd ers dros 70 mlynedd. Last episode: an emotional reunion for Ian who's searched for his sister for over 10yrs.

programme.title: Garddio A Mwy

programme.desc: Dylan Iorwerth visits a small village in Italy for the opening of a museum to commemorate the work of Benjamin Piercy. Piercy's name is not familiar in modern day Wales but he made a huge contribution here a century and a half ago.

programme.title: Chwedlau Tinga Tinga
programme.desc: There was a time when Elephant had no nose. He couldn't swat away the flies, or scrub his belly and he couldn't even blow his nose. Then one day snappy Crocodile snapped. programme.title: Street Fighter

programme.title: Spider-Man: Homecoming
programme.desc: Under the watchful eye of mentor Tony Stark, high school student Peter Parker starts to embrace his newfound identity as Spider-Man. When the evil Vulture emerges to cause havoc, everything Peter loves is in jeopardy.

programme.title: Spider-Man: No Way Home
programme.desc: Spider-Man's identity is revealed to everyone, and he can no longer separate his normal life from his superhero life. When he asks Doctor Strange for help, it forces him to discover what it means to be him.

programme.title: Shazam!
programme.desc: Billy Batson is a streetwise 14-year-old who can magically transform into the adult superhero Shazam simply by shouting out one word. His newfound powers soon get put to the test when he squares off against the evil Dr. Thaddeus Sivana.

programme.title: The Italian Job
programme.desc: A group of petty criminals plan to rig a major traffic jam to divert attention from their bank heist.

programme.desc: From killers and zombies to forbidden love and dangerous desires, `The Cinema List' explores romances where passion courts danger and love is always worth the risk.

programme.title: Anchorman: The Legend of Ron Burgundy
programme.desc: Knockabout comedy set in the sexist world of 1970s newscasting. Ron Burgundy, San Diego's top-rated anchorman, reigns supreme in the all-male world of television news, until the arrival of an ambitious female reporter upsets the status quo. When she steps in to present the programme in Ron's absence, an uncivil war breaks out in the newsroom.

programme.title: Blades of Glory
programme.desc: Chazz Michael Michaels and Jimmy MacElroy are the two titans of skating. At the Winter Olympics, the fierce rivals find themselves forced to share the winner's podium when they both score gold. The shower of glory doesn't last for long, as the pair descend into fisticuffs and set the mascot on fire. Disgraced and stripped of their medals, they can get back on ice, but only if they dance with each other as a pair.

programme.title: Dumb & Dumber
programme.desc: A pair of idiotic friends set off on a madcap journey to return a briefcase to its owner. However, they are blissfully unaware that the case contains a large amount of ransom money for the owner's kidnapped husband. Their trip is soon fraught with disaster, especially when the kidnappers begin to pursue them across the country with the aim of grabbing the cash.

programme.title: Dumb and Dumberer: When Harry Met Lloyd
programme.desc: A grant-greedy principal puts two clueless teens in a bogus class for students with special needs.

programme.title: Two Weeks Notice
programme.desc: An ambitious law graduate decides to leave her job as she feels her skills are not appreciated by her boss, who basically treats her like his PA. However, he won't let her go that easily.

programme.title: Beverly Hills Cop
programme.desc: A fast-talking cop trails his best friend's killer from the alleys of Detroit to the posh galleries of Beverly Hills.

programme.title: Bridget Jones: Mad About the Boy
programme.desc: After jumping back into the dating pool, single mother Bridget Jones finds herself caught between a younger man and her son's science teacher.

programme.title: American Pie
programme.desc: Believing it essential before they go to college, four teenage boys vow to lose their virginity and enter into a pact, each attempting to be the first to do the deed as their efforts are met with varying levels of success and failure.

programme.title: Miss Congeniality
programme.desc: An uncouth FBI agent goes undercover in the Miss United States beauty pageant as a contestant to flush out a terrorist who plans to bomb the event. The hard-nosed cop, who prides herself on her toughness, must discard her trainers and feminise her behaviour if she is to avoid blowing her cover.

programme.title: Miss Congeniality 2: Armed and Fabulous
programme.desc: FBI agent Gracie is given another makeover to become the face of the Bureau, prior to embarking on a promotional tour. When two of her friends are kidnapped - Miss USA and a beauty pageant director - she decides to investigate on her own, in defiance of orders to the contrary.

programme.title: The Cable Guy
programme.desc: After being kicked out of his apartment by his girlfriend, Steven hears that if he slips a cable guy $50, he can get hooked up with free cable - but at what further cost? Can Steven gently reject the cable guy's insane attempts to be best buds and also get his girlfriend back?

programme.title: Sausage Party
programme.desc: Supermarket perishables devise a plan to escape from their human enemies after Frank the sausage learns that they will eventually become meals.

programme.title: Police Academy

programme.title: An Officer and a Gentleman

programme.title: The End of the Affair
programme.desc: As war rages over London, neighbours Maurice Bendrix and Sarah Miles fall in love and begin a relationship. Unfortunately Sarah is married to Henry, a work colleague of the American Maurice, who confides in him that he thinks his wife is cheating on him.

programme.title: The Breakfast Club

programme.title: Cookster: The Darkest Days
programme.desc: A dyslexic teen misunderstood by his family and desperate for respect, becomes a drug dealer struggling with addiction and debt while a rival threatens to destroy his world, driving him apart from the woman he loves.

programme.title: A Good Person
programme.desc: After surviving a tragedy, a woman emerges from recovery with an opioid addiction and unresolved grief. However, her unlikely friendship with her would-be father-in-law gives her a fighting chance to put her life back together.

programme.title: Downton Abbey: The Grand Finale
programme.desc: In the summer of 1930, the Crawleys grapple with the threat of social disgrace when Mary finds herself at the centre of a public scandal and the family face financial trouble.

programme.desc: A woman gets into a taxi at New York airport and gradually tells the driver about her unfortunate decisions that led to an affair with a married man. The driver also reveals more and more about his life.

programme.title: Cookster: The Darkest Days
programme.desc: A dyslexic teen misunderstood by his family and desperate for respect, becomes a drug dealer struggling with addiction and debt while a rival threatens to destroy his world, driving him apart from the woman he loves.

programme.title: The End of the Affair
programme.desc: On a rainy London night in 1946, novelist Maurice Bendrix has a chance meeting with Henry Miles, the husband of his ex-mistress Sarah, who abruptly ended their affair two years before. Bendrix's obsession with Sarah is rekindled; he succumbs to his own jealousy and arranges to have her followed. As the investigation progresses, we learn the reason for their separation.

programme.title: Super-Charlie
programme.desc: A young boy attempts to solve an ongoing series of crimes with the help of his super-powered baby brother.

programme.title: The Portable Door
programme.desc: A man lands an internship at a mysterious London firm with unconventional employees, including the charismatic CEO who is incorporating modern corporate strategy into ancient magical practices.

programme.title: Buffalo Kids
programme.desc: Arriving in New York City in 1886, two orphaned Irish siblings embark on a wild cross-country journey aboard a transcontinental train. They make a new friend and encounter heroes and villains in their quest to find a new home.

programme.title: Scooby-Doo
programme.desc: Two years on from going their separate ways after solving their last case, the Mystery Inc crew reunite to investigate the strange goings-on at an amusement park called Spooky Island. The attraction appears to lure its young visitors in very strange ways and it is up to Scooby, Shaggy and the gang to get to the bottom of the mystery.

programme.title: Scooby-Doo 2: Monsters Unleashed
programme.desc: The crime-busting gang, Mystery Inc, and their dopey mutt Scooby-Doo are back, this time investigating the spooky goings-on brought about by an evil genius intent on wreaking havoc in the town of Coolsville.

programme.title: Paddington in Peru
programme.desc: Paddington and his adopted family, the Browns, decide to visit his aunt Lucy in Peru, but circumstances lead them towards misadventures in the Amazon rainforest and mountains of Peru.

programme.title: How to Train Your Dragon
programme.desc: On the isle of Berk, a Viking boy named Hiccup defies tradition by befriending a dragon named Toothless. However, when an ancient threat emerges that endangers both species, Hiccup's friendship with Toothless becomes the key to forging a new future.

programme.title: Pokémon Detective Pikachu
programme.desc: A young man joins forces with Detective Pikachu to unravel the mystery behind his father's disappearance. Chasing clues through the streets of Ryme City, the dynamic duo soon discover a devious plot that poses a threat to the Pokémon universe.

programme.title: Jumanji: Welcome to the Jungle
programme.desc: Four teens embark on the adventure of a lifetime when a video game magically transports them to the jungle and transforms them into adults.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog 3
programme.desc: Sonic, Knuckles and Tails reunite to battle Shadow, a mysterious new enemy with powers unlike anything they've faced before. With their abilities outmatched in every way, they seek out an unlikely alliance to stop Shadow and protect the planet.

programme.title: Super-Charlie
programme.desc: A young boy attempts to solve an ongoing series of crimes with the help of his super-powered baby brother.

programme.title: The Ant Bully
programme.desc: This is the story of a ten-year-old boy named Lucas Nickle, who has just moved to a new neighbourhood, has no friends, and is the target for the local bully and his gang. His parents are leaving for a honeymoon in Puerto Vallarta, so they are too busy to attend to his problems. His sister is distracted by her cellular phone, and the grandmother is obsessed with UFOs and aliens.

programme.title: The Last Airbender
programme.desc: Following the adventures of Yang who is a successor in to a long line of Avatars. He has to put aside his childhood in order to stop he Fire Nation from enslaving the Water, Earth and Air nations.

programme.title: The Last Airbender
programme.desc: Following the adventures of Yang who is a successor in to a long line of Avatars. He has to put aside his childhood in order to stop he Fire Nation from enslaving the Water, Earth and Air nations.

programme.title: Gremlins 2: The New Batch
programme.desc: Six years after defeating the Gremlins, Billy and Kate have grown up and moved to New York. However, Billy stumbles across Gizmo in a genetics lab and when he gets wet, all hell breaks loose, and the stage is set for more deliciously bad taste humour.

programme.title: The Cinema List: Book Adaptations
programme.desc: From page to screen, the Cinema List celebrates book adaptations. Featuring Wuthering Heights starring Margot Robbie and Bridget Jones's Diary, see these stories on film.

programme.title: Days of Thunder
programme.desc: A hotshot rookie stock car driver sets his sights on the professional NASCAR circuit and the prestigious Daytona 500.

programme.title: Jaws 2
programme.desc: Another great white shark arrives to terrorise the holiday resort of Amity Island. Having recovered from the grisly events of four years ago, the island is once again packed with tourists. Families are sailing in the bay and swimming off the beach, but when two divers and a water-skier go missing, the local police chief fears that he is about to relive his greatest nightmare.

programme.title: Jaws III
programme.desc: Second sequel to Steven Spielberg's 1970s blockbuster transports the action from Amity Island to a new undersea kingdom attraction in a Florida theme park. The capture of a baby great white shark is big news and brings crowds to the park - until its mother arrives on the scene.

programme.title: Jurassic Park
programme.desc: On a remote island, a wealthy entrepreneur creates a theme park featuring living dinosaurs drawn from prehistoric DNA. Before opening the attraction to the public, he invites a top palaeontologist, palaeobotanist, mathematician/theorist, and his two eager grandchildren to experience the park. Their park visit is anything but tranquil as the park's security system breaks down, and the prehistoric creatures break out.

programme.title: The Lost World: Jurassic Park
programme.desc: Sequel in which the entrepreneur who genetically engineered the dinosaurs in Jurassic Park transpires to have cloned more of the prehistoric monsters on another island location. Researchers arrive to observe the creatures in their natural habitat, but hot on their heels is a band of mercenaries intent on capturing the dinosaurs and transporting them back to San Diego Zoo.

programme.title: Jumanji
programme.desc: Alan Parrish disappeared as a young boy. Nobody believes his friend's story that he was sucked into the game they were playing. Twenty-six years later, two children find the game in their attic, and when they start to play, Alan is released, but the game isn't over yet, and Alan must finish before he is really free.

programme.title: First Knight
programme.desc: Dissatisfied with King Arthur's rule of peace and freedom, Malagant, an erstwhile ally, turns against the Round Table and establishes a hostile domain. However, he goes too far when he kidnaps the queen and imprisons her in an appalling dungeon. The special skills of the glittering and impetuous swordsman Lancelot must be called upon to effect her rescue.

programme.title: Bad Boys
programme.desc: Two wisecracking Miami cops are forced to swap identities in order to track down $100 million of heroin stolen from the police. Their only lead is a young woman who can identify the culprits, but when they track her down, she mistakes one of the cops for his partner, forcing the pair into a bizarre and chaotic life swap.

programme.title: Demolition Man
programme.desc: Violence has been outlawed in the politically correct regime of 2032, and the only weapons in existence are consigned to a museum, but when an escaped psychopath heads straight for the museum, the authorities decide the only effective recourse they have is to thaw out a violent, cryogenically frozen cop.

programme.title: Shenandoah
programme.desc: A man's neutrality during the American Civil War is tested by his daughter's marriage and his son's capture by Union forces.

programme.title: High Plains Drifter
programme.desc: A mysterious stranger rides into a small frontier town in the American southwest, provoked into a gunfight in which he kills three men, and then begged by the townsfolk to become sheriff. The residents are terrified of the gunslinger but live in greater fear of a gang which intends to wipe out the whole town.

programme.title: Mr. Nice Guy
programme.desc: Martial arts romp about a TV chef who becomes embroiled in a drug lord's search for an incriminating video. The kung-fu fighting cook steps in to save an investigative journalist on the run from a gang of murderous drug dealers, resulting in a potentially fatal mix-up involving her incriminating tape of the gangsters and a children's video.

programme.title: One Last Deal
programme.desc: A veteran sports agent races to secure a final lucrative contract for his star client, as both their careers and lives hang in the balance. Starring Danny Dyer.

programme.title: The Bride!

programme.title: Him

programme.channel: Sky Cinema Sci Fi & Horror HD.uk

programme.channel: Sky Cinema Sci Fi & Horror HD.uk

programme.channel: Sky Cinema Sci Fi & Horror HD.uk
programme.title: The Lawnmower Man
programme.desc: Visually arresting thriller about a researcher who uses a mentally disabled gardener for ill-advised experiments in inducing mental development through virtual reality simulations. At first, the simulations seem to enhance the lawnmower man's mental powers, but then the experiment goes haywire. Based on a short story by Stephen King.

programme.channel: Sky Cinema Sci Fi & Horror HD.uk
programme.title: Ghostbusters

programme.channel: Sky Cinema Sci Fi & Horror HD.uk

programme.desc: The secretive Men in Black are agents charged with regulating the aliens who have settled on Earth and ensure that they remain undetected. programme.channel: Sky Cinema Sci Fi & Horror HD.uk

programme.channel: Sky Cinema Sci Fi & Horror HD.uk

programme.channel: Sky Cinema Sci Fi & Horror HD.uk

Suddenly the men find their lives are in grave danger as they are stalked by a cowboy robot who will stop at nothing to eliminate them.

programme.title: The Abyss - Rise and Fall of the Nazis
programme.desc: In the wake of the First World War, Adolf Hitler identifies Benito Mussolini as his model and has one ultimate goal: to gain power in Germany.

programme.title: The Abyss - Rise and Fall of the Nazis
programme.desc: Hitler appears to be eliminated after his failed putsch in 1923. Discover how the Nazi movement used democracy's tolerance in Germany to come back.

programme.title: The Abyss - Rise and Fall of the Nazis
programme.desc: A protest turns into a racist and anti-Jewish movement during the 1920s and the NSDAP use emotions to gain the loyalty of the people.

programme.title: The Abyss - Rise and Fall of the Nazis

programme.title: World War II With Tom Hanks
programme.desc: The story of how America's battle to control the seas against the terrifying German U-boats in the Atlantic and Japan's fearsome Navy in the Pacific ultimately turns the tide of the war.

programme.title: Mysteries Unearthed With Danny Trejo
programme.desc: A species thought lost forever is spotted again. A dog plays an unexpected role in recovering the World Cup trophy. And a puppy from the Ice Age emerges intact after 18,000 years. These stories reveal just how unpredictable the natural world can be.

programme.title: The UnBelievable With Dan Aykroyd
programme.desc: Some of history's most brilliant innovations are also the most bizarre. Like a fully functioning car that hovers in midair. Or clothing made out of everyone's favourite source of calcium. These are the tales of history's most bizarre inventions.

programme.title: The UnBelievable With Dan Aykroyd

programme.desc: It's the end of November in the Luangwa Valley, and the rains have just begun. It marks the end of one of the toughest dry season on record, and triggers a transition from parched wilderness to luscious wetland.

programme.title: Zambia Untamed
programme.desc: In the Luangwa Valley lies a grove of Ebony trees, home to a troop of baboons. But, there are dangers here as the resident leopard is powerful and agile enough to climb trees.

programme.title: Into the Wild Colombia
programme.desc: Growing up in the rainforests in the heart of Colombia, a young capuchin monkey has to master his climbing skills, learn how to find food and to distinguish friend from foe.

programme.title: Secrets of Wild Australia
programme.desc: The snakes of Australia are as diverse and striking as the landscape they inhabit. There are more than 300 different species here, some harmless to humans, others deadly.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: The next stage of lemur introductions keeps the primate care staff on their toes, while gibbons Kim and Tien head back to their familiar stomping ground.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: In Dao Tien a group of misbehaving doucs need to be relocated and female golden-cheeked gibbon Tungs release is hampered by the weather.

programme.title: Dawn to Dusk
programme.desc: The sun rules everything on this arid peninsula, especially in July. But those who call this desert home, such as camels, lizards and rock hyraxes, have adapted to beat the heat.

programme.title: High Life in the Dead Mountains

programme.desc: The snakes of Australia are as diverse and striking as the landscape they inhabit. There are more than 300 different species here, some harmless to humans, others deadly.

programme.title: Brazil Untamed
programme.desc: A look at the wildlife of Brazil.

programme.title: Waterworld Africa
programme.desc: A subtropical realm of staggering diversity exists on the eastern coast of Africa. A mangrove forest experiences the high and low tides.

programme.title: Dawn to Dusk
programme.desc: The sun rules everything on this arid peninsula, especially in July. But those who call this desert home, such as camels, lizards and rock hyraxes, have adapted to beat the heat.

programme.title: High Life in the Dead Mountains
Orangutan Jungle School
programme.desc: Introducing some of the big characters including `class clown' Valentino and `Big Boy' Beni. There is also an emergency arrival with teenage mum Clara and her baby Clarita and vets round up the older orangutans, who are now ready to be released.

programme.title: Britannia
programme.desc: Divis realises that Love was sent by Harka, and is heartbroken that he has failed the prophecy. Meanwhile, Phelan wanders through the forest hoping to atone for his actions.

programme.title: Most Haunted
programme.desc: Medium Johnnie Firori looks for spooks at Coalhouse Fort in Essex, a site believed to be a hotbed of paranormal activity such as mists, orbs and sudden drops in room temperature.

programme.title: Most Haunted
programme.desc: The team spend the night at Morecambe Gardens, where many ghosts are said to reside. The ghosts are supposedly split into the two halves of the garden, with `good' spirits haunting one side and `evil' spirits haunting the other. programme.desc: Following rapid response officers, the fastest cops in Britain, as they crack down on crime.

programme.title: A1: Highway Patrol

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: A thief raids a social club by sneaking in and waiting for the staff to go home, a computer shop owner turns detective to track down a burglar and a couple are shocked when someone steals from their farm for rescued animals.

programme.title: Mortimer and Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul and Bob travel to the picturesque River Usk in Wales, where Paul's father first taught him to fish as a young child. Their quarry is the wild brown trout. They discuss their respective childhoods and reflect on their own family lives. In their cosy cottage accommodation in the heart of the countryside they compare school photographs and Paul serenades Bob with his guitar.

programme.title: Mortimer and Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul and Bob brave the wild lakes of Essex in search of the mighty carp. They have the latest high tech equipment and are fully prepared to stay all night if they have to, to catch the biggest fish they can.

programme.title: Mortimer and Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Bob has harboured a childhood dream to catch a salmon so they set off for The River Tay in Scotland full of hope and excitement for this special trip. programme.title: Mortimer and Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul and Bob fly to Enniskillen to fish for Pike. They take to the waters of Lough Erne in their search for this mighty fish, guided by the very experienced local Frenchman Pascal. They discuss their respective bad habits and Bob gives Paul a thorough health update, before they retire to a local hostelry to sample the traditional Irish refreshments.

programme.title: Mortimer and Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul and Bob fish for the graceful Grayling on the stunning River Ure in the heart of the Yorkshire Dales. programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornish Christmas
programme.desc: Rick Stein joins in the ancient festival of Wassailing, tries out the famous ice rink at Eden, enjoys the company of Port Isaac's Fisherman's Friends and cooks his own goose. programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The lads rev it up when they race a BMW 6 Series, Porsche 911 and Jaguar XKR along Pendine Sands in Wales. programme.title: Mortimer and Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul and Bob decide to try and catch a legendary Pike, which is perhaps not the best idea for two men of a certain age with heart problems. In the cosy cottage where they are staying, they invite a consultant cardiologist for dinner to talk about their medical past and their future, gaining tips on how to live the most healthy life possible. Facing the future, they write a eulogy for each other as the sun sets on their final fishing expedition.

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Dave and Si look across the Channel to see how our French cousins have influenced the way we cook and eat over the centuries. programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Lucille is seconded to St Cuthbert's when a ward Sister is off sick. Lucille will supervise the pupil midwives and brush up on her hospital care. While there, Lucille experiences hostility from a mother to be, Connie, who is begrudgingly on bed rest because her waters have broken early.

programme.title: Catherine Cookson Dramas
programme.desc: After losing her parents to cholera, young Cissie fights to keep her five young siblings out of the workhouse. However her life becomes more complicated when the wealthy Fischel family take an unwelcome interest in her.

programme.title: Home Fires
programme.desc: Drama series set in a Cheshire village during the Second World War. The isolated community could not feel further away from the impending bloodshed and battlefields, yet it is not immune from the effects of war. Everyone must make sacrifices as war starts to have an impact on the village. As Pat's marriage deteriorates, Kate and Jack's relationship intensifies.

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: Hetty is drawn to a small, country village by the apparent suicide of Robert's uncle. All is not as it seems. What evil caused the old man's death?

programme.title: Sister Boniface Mysteries
programme.desc: A sighting of the mysteriously missing Baron Battenberg sends the press and police into a frenzy; determined to uncover the facts, Ruth and Sam go undercover at the Pranayama retreat, run by the Baron's widow Marion Grey and brother Edwin Battenberg.

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: An American diplomat is shot dead in a neighbourhood in the centre of London. While analysing crime pictures and the positions of the bodies of the victims, the team discovers a secret that reaches the pinnacle of the American government.

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: An eccentric group of ex-police officers are brought out of retirement to investigate unsolved crimes. Lane's future at UCOS is threatened by a death in custody which emerges from his past. Meanwhile, the discovery of a mysterious gun in the Thames re-opens the unsolved 1998 murder of a playboy shipping heir and leads the team to the Rock of Gibraltar.

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Leading up to Mother's Day, the team must survive Lent and are forced to give up more than just cigarettes and sugar. A thief has been pinching their milk as well. Meanwhile, a gentle romance is blooming between Miss Higgins and Sergeant Woolf.

programme.title: 2point4 Children

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Heather takes the first step in her new life, Glenda finds a way to make the working day a little bit more interesting and Alfie has a surprise in store for Kat.

programme.title: Tenko
programme.desc: Captured by the Japanese and separated from their men, the women are marched to their internment camp and cannot believe what they find there.

programme.title: The Doctor Blake Mysteries

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: Lovejoy uncovers a rather unpleasant case of blackmail when a teenage girl brings him some 18th-century erotic drawings to sell.

programme.title: Waiting for God

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine

programme.desc: When Ivy and Nora reveal that a childhood school friend is returning from Australia, Truly suddenly becomes overwhelmed with emotion.

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine

programme.title: The Bill

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: When PC Smith makes a wrongful arrest, it helps him to see that life isn't just black and white.

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Glaze and Holmes investigate what appears to be the suicide of a local councillor, but appearances can be deceptive.

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Leading up to Mother's Day, the team must survive Lent and are forced to give up more than just cigarettes and sugar. A thief has been pinching their milk as well. Meanwhile, a gentle romance is blooming between Miss Higgins and Sergeant Woolf.

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Lucille is seconded to St Cuthbert's when a ward Sister is off sick. Lucille will supervise the pupil midwives and brush up on her hospital care. While there, Lucille experiences hostility from a mother to be, Connie, who is begrudgingly on bed rest because her waters have broken early.

programme.title: Catherine Cookson Dramas
programme.desc: After losing her parents to cholera, young Cissie fights to keep her five young siblings out of the workhouse. However her life becomes more complicated when the wealthy Fischel family take an unwelcome interest in her.

programme.title: Home Fires
programme.desc: Drama series set in a Cheshire village during the Second World War. The isolated community could not feel further away from the impending bloodshed and battlefields, yet it is not immune from the effects of war. Everyone must make sacrifices as war starts to have an impact on the village. As Pat's marriage deteriorates, Kate and Jack's relationship intensifies.

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: Hetty is drawn to a small, country village by the apparent suicide of Robert's uncle. All is not as it seems. What evil caused the old man's death?

programme.title: Sister Boniface Mysteries
programme.desc: A sighting of the mysteriously missing Baron Battenberg sends the press and police into a frenzy; determined to uncover the facts, Ruth and Sam go undercover at the Pranayama retreat, run by the Baron's widow Marion Grey and brother Edwin Battenberg.

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: An American diplomat is shot dead in a neighbourhood in the centre of London. While analysing crime pictures and the positions of the bodies of the victims, the team discovers a secret that reaches the pinnacle of the American government.

programme.title: Wild Scandinavia
programme.desc: Exploring the Scandinavian forest, an ancient land shrouded in myth and legend, and its animals, including wolves, lynx, osprey and reindeer.

programme.title: Wild Scandinavia
programme.desc: Exploring Scandinavia's kingdoms of fire and ice and the animals -- including muskoxen, polar bears and arctic foxes -- who must navigate the landscape as it changes around them.

programme.title: New Zealand: Earth's Mythical Islands
programme.desc: Isolated since the time of the dinosaurs, New Zealand's wildlife has been left to its own devices - with surprising consequences. Its ancient forests are still stalked by predators from the Jurassic era. It's also one of the most geologically active countries on Earth. From Kiwis with their giant eggs, to forest-dwelling penguins and helicopter-riding sheep dogs, meet the astonishing creatures and resilient people who must rise to the challenges of their beautiful, dramatic and demanding home.

programme.title: New Zealand: Earth's Mythical Islands
programme.desc: The most extreme and wild parts of New Zealand are in the South Island, which lie towards Antarctica, in the path of the tempestuous `roaring forties'. This is home to some of the most rapidly rising mountains in the world, the Southern Alps. From hyper-intelligent parrots, to sinister snails with teeth and magical constellations of glow-worms, this is the story of New Zealand's wildest places and its most resilient pioneers, all of who must embrace radical solutions to survive.

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: Terry has just got out of the army and tries calling up all his ex girlfriends, without any luck.

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: Bob Ferris organises a surprise birthday party for Terry, but arrangements to pick him up from the pub as usual go pear-shaped.

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: This memorable storyline has Bob and Terry going to extraordinary lengths to avoid hearing a football score before they watch a recording of the match on TV. Interestingly, those other two Geordie lads Ant and Dec made their own version of this particular episode.

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: Bob thinks Terry needs new friends, but, by inviting him to a trendy dinner party, he also invites disaster. Terry though, finds an unlikely ally in Thelma.

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses...
programme.desc: Rodney gets an unexpected windfall and decides to take Cassandra on the holiday of a lifetime in Miami, unaware that the non-refundable tickets are for the day of her bank's seminar in Eastbourne. In a show of brotherly love, Del naturally offers to accompany Rodders, and so they bid farewell to Peckham and embark on a holiday they are likely never to forget.

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses...
programme.sub-title: Miami Twice: Oh, to Be in England
programme.desc: With Del handling the accommodation and transport for their week's holiday in America, it's not surprising that he and Rodney end up in a dodgy camper-van. Things get even worse when the van disappears while they are in a nightclub.

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: Alice solves the problem of the Christmas show by proposing a promenading Nativity, in which she and Hugo will play Mary and Joseph. However, when her waters break mid-performance, she has to check with Geraldine whether she has actually given birth to the baby Jesus.

programme.desc: The Bishop of Mulberry is to officiate at the christening of Alice and Hugo's baby. Geraldine and Owen are to be the godparents while Frank and Jim act as god-grandparents. When an amazed David finds that he and Geraldine are well matched, he asks her to marry him.

programme.title: Terry and June
programme.desc: When the landlord of the local pub is advised to take a rest by his doctor, Terry and June offer to run the place in his absence. However, their good deed looks set to backfire when Sir Dennis threatens to sack anyone found guilty of moonlighting.

programme.title: Terry and June
programme.desc: Terry loves to be the centre of attention, but when he discovers he bears an uncanny resemblance to a criminal, he wishes the spotlight would focus on someone else.

programme.title: Terry and June
programme.desc: In this episode, the couple are moving to Purley but plan to redecorate the house before moving in - until they learn of the sinister reputation of their new home.

programme.desc: When it comes to moving house, Terry once again bites off more than he can chew.

programme.desc: Terry gets into a lather when a new carpet fails to arrive in time for a visit from his boss. The neighbours help him precipitate disaster.

programme.desc: Robbie takes a tumble at Buckby Locks in Northamptonshire and has a problem with his power supply and battery bank.

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Ben and Susan take part in Michael's school fundraising auction. However, Ben quickly regrets making a pledge when he spies a former patient with severe halitosis bidding for a double session in his dentist's chair.

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Susan has taken to asking an increasingly disturbed Michael for advice on make-up and clothes now that Janey has left for university. The arrival of Ben's cousin's daughter in the Harper household could provide a solution - but Ben is reluctant to give up the peace and quiet he has spent his entire married life longing for.

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: The Harpers star in a documentary about family life as part of Abi's latest college assignment. Susan is happy to present a facade of domestic bliss and dispense cookery tips along with relationship advice. Ben, however, is more concerned with catching a rogue rodent.

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Ben and Susan look forward to a night out in a town where nobody knows their names when they visit daughter Janey, who is away at university. Booking into a hotel under the names of Johnny Studd and Delores de Large, the stage is set for a night full of surprises, of one kind or another.

programme.title: My Family
programme.sub-title: The Second Greatest Story Ever Told
programme.desc: Susan's feathers are ruffled when Nick lands the plum part of Jesus in an amateur theatrical production, leaving Susan to play the rather less demanding part of Shepherd Number Two. Ben, meanwhile, has an extremely painful toothache to contend with, and is wary of entrusting his dental needs to any other dentist.

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: After Susan finds marijuana in Michael's room, which he got from his friend Bex, she decides to teach him a lesson but finds herself scrambling after she `confiscates' the evidence.

programme.desc: Contestants play a large coin-drop machine, provided they answer questions correctly. Answer incorrectly and the players could be eliminated. The last contestant standing has an opportunity to win the machine's £10,000 jackpot.
